body:
## Summary

Fixes #1700.

`/log` and `/terminal_watch` rejected every screen / tmux / docker session with `❌ This command currently supports only sessions launched with $ isolation`, even when `$ --status <uuid>` on the host showed a normal record (`status executed`, `logPath …`, `options { isolated screen, isolationMode detached, … }`).

Root cause: `parseSessionStatusOutput` looked for the isolation backend at `data.isolation` and `data.options.isolation`, but [`link-foundation/start`](https://github.com/link-foundation/start) 0.25.x reports it at `options.isolated` in both JSON output and the default `links-notation` output.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/isolation-runner.lib.mjs` | Read `options.isolated` first; keep `options.isolation` and top-level `isolation` as fallbacks. Update both JSON and text/links-notation branches. |
| `src/telegram-log-command.lib.mjs`, `src/telegram-terminal-watch-command.lib.mjs` | Emit a `[VERBOSE]` diagnostic at the rejection site exposing `parsedIsolation`, `sessionInfoBackend`, and the first 240 chars of the raw `$ --status` output, so future contract drifts can be triaged from a single bot log line. |
| `tests/test-issue-1700-isolation-parsing.mjs` | Regression test built directly from the `$ --status` capture in the issue. Fails on the unfixed parser, passes after the fix. Covers links-notation + JSON for screen / tmux / docker. |
| `.changeset/fix-isolation-parser-options-isolated-1700.md` | Patch changeset. |
| `docs/case-studies/issue-1700/` | Issue data, screenshots, raw `$ --status` capture, timeline, requirements, root cause analysis, and research sources (link-foundation/start source files inspected). |

## Reproducer

The issue body contains the exact `$ --status` output. The minimum reproducer (`tests/test-issue-1700-isolation-parsing.mjs`) feeds it to `parseSessionStatusOutput` → `decideLogDestination`:

```js
const parsed = parseSessionStatusOutput(realOutput);
parsed.isolation; // before: null    after: "screen"

const decision = decideLogDestination({ statusResult: parsed, /* … */ });
decision.destination; // before: "reject"   after: "chat"
```

## Verification

- `node tests/test-issue-1700-isolation-parsing.mjs` — 14/14
- `node tests/test-issue-1686-log-command.mjs` — 45/45
- `node tests/test-isolation-runner.mjs` — 15/15
- `node tests/test-extract-isolation-from-args.mjs` — 26/26
- `node tests/test-isolation-screen-fallback-1545.mjs` — 5/5
- `npm test` — 65/65 default-suite test files
- `npm run lint` — clean

## Test plan

- [x] Regression test reproduces the bug, then passes after the fix.
- [x] `npm test` passes locally.
- [x] `npm run lint` passes.
- [ ] Manual end-to-end smoke test on a deployed bot: launch a `$ --isolated screen` session, reply with `/log` and `/terminal_watch`, confirm both accept the session.

## Out of scope

- Streaming `tail -f` updates of a live session log (already tracked elsewhere).
- Auto-uploading logs > 50 MB (already tracked elsewhere).
- Renaming `parseSessionStatusOutput`'s output field from `isolation` to `isolated` to match upstream — keep the existing API surface to avoid touching every consumer.
title: Fix /log + /terminal_watch rejecting real $ isolation sessions (#1700)

body:
## Summary

Fixes #1720.

`/terminal_watch` was uploading the full session log file at the end of
each watch via `bot.telegram.sendDocument(chatId, ...)`. Two problems
with that:

- It duplicates the dedicated `/log` command — the only command the
  bot exposes for delivering session logs.
- The raw `bot.telegram.sendDocument(chatId, ...)` call carries no
  `message_thread_id`, so in forum-enabled supergroups the document
  lands in the General topic instead of the topic where the watch was
  invoked, with no reply chain — see the screenshot in the issue.

This PR removes the duplicate upload from `/terminal_watch` (and the
helper that did it). The final `✅ Terminal watch complete` message
edit is preserved, so users still see the session reached terminal
status; for the log file they use `/log <uuid>`, which already replies
in the correct topic via `ctx.replyWithDocument` (Telegraf injects
`message_thread_id` automatically).

`/log` itself was already correct — it uses `ctx.replyWithDocument(...)`
with `reply_to_message_id`, which Telegraf annotates with
`message_thread_id` from the originating message. A new regression
test asserts this so future refactors do not silently regress
topic/thread handling.

## Reproduction

1. Configure the Telegram bot with a forum-enabled supergroup.
2. From any topic in that supergroup, run a long-running session via
   the bot so a session UUID is reported (`📊 Session: <uuid>`).
3. Run `/terminal_watch <uuid>` (or reply to the status message with
   `/terminal_watch`).
4. Wait until the session reaches a terminal status (e.g. `executed`).

**Before fix**: a `<uuid>.log` document is uploaded into the General
topic, with no reply chain (issue screenshot).
**After fix**: the watch message is updated to `✅ Terminal watch
complete` and **no** log document is uploaded by `/terminal_watch`.
Use `/log <uuid>` to fetch the log; it replies in the correct topic.

## Files changed

- `src/telegram-terminal-watch-command.lib.mjs` — drop the log upload
  on completion; remove `sendLogDocument`, `attachLogOnComplete`, and
  the unused `path` / `fsConstants` / `TELEGRAM_DOCUMENT_MAX_BYTES`
  imports.
- `tests/test-issue-467-terminal-watch.mjs` — assert
  `documents.length === 0` after a watch completes.
- `tests/test-issue-1720-terminal-watch-no-log.mjs` — new regression
  test covering both `/terminal_watch` (no upload) and `/log`
  (replies to the originating message → Telegraf carries the topic).
- `.changeset/fix-terminal-watch-log-upload-1720.md` — patch
  changeset for the next release.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-1720/` — case study with the original
  screenshot, raw issue/PR data, and the timeline / root-cause
  analysis required by the issue.

## Test plan

- [x] `node tests/test-issue-1720-terminal-watch-no-log.mjs` — 7/7 pass
- [x] `node tests/test-issue-467-terminal-watch.mjs` — 18/18 pass
- [x] `node tests/test-issue-1686-log-command.mjs` — 45/45 pass
- [x] `npm run lint` — clean
- [x] `npx prettier --check` — clean

## Screenshot from the issue

The case study folder contains the original screenshot at
`docs/case-studies/issue-1720/screenshots/issue-screenshot.png`:

![Issue screenshot](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1720-2101de719cd1/docs/case-studies/issue-1720/screenshots/issue-screenshot.png?raw=true)

That `938a9d28-…log` document is exactly what this PR stops uploading.
title: Fix /terminal_watch uploading log file in General topic (#1720)

body:
## Summary

Fixes #467.

This PR adds live terminal log watching for Telegram bot sessions on the current `main` codebase.

- Adds `/terminal_watch <uuid>` and reply-based `/terminal_watch` for `$ --status` isolation sessions.
- Updates a separate Telegram message with the latest terminal-sized log snapshot, then freezes it and attaches the full log when the session reaches a terminal status.
- Supports `--size WIDTHxHEIGHT`, `--width`, `--height`, `--interval-ms`, and `--max-chars` options.
- Keeps `--auto-start-screen-watch-message` experimental and off by default; auto-start only creates watch messages for public `/solve` sessions and skips private or unknown-visibility repositories.
- Reuses `/log` privacy behavior for manual watches: public logs can update in chat, private/unknown visibility is delivered by DM.
- Documents the terminal watch feature in the English, Hindi, Russian, and Chinese root README and `docs/CONFIGURATION` variants.
- Adds `tests/test-docs-language-sync.mjs` to validate that root README and top-level `docs/*.md` language siblings are updated together, and wires it into the `validate-docs` CI job.
- Extracts `safeReply` from `telegram-bot.mjs` so the bot file remains below the 1500-line CI limit.
- Merges the branch with current `main` to resolve the previous dirty merge state.

## How to Reproduce

1. Start the bot with default screen isolation.
2. Run `/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123` in an authorized group.
3. Use the session UUID from the bot's execution message:
   - `/terminal_watch <uuid>`
   - or reply to the session message with `/terminal_watch --size 120x25`
4. Optional auto-start path: start the bot with `--auto-start-screen-watch-message` and run `/solve` against a public repository.

## Validation

- `npm run format:check`
- `npm run lint`
- `npm run check:duplication`
- `npm test`
- `node tests/docs-validation.mjs`
- `node tests/test-docs-language-sync.mjs`
- `node tests/test-docs-options-sync.mjs`
- `node tests/test-issue-467-terminal-watch.mjs`
- `node tests/test-issue-1686-log-command.mjs`
- `node tests/test-telegram-bot-dry-run.mjs`
- `node tests/test-isolation-runner.mjs`
- `bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh`
- `git diff --check`
title: Add Telegram terminal watch command

body:
## Summary

Implements issue #524 — gives users a way to cancel a specific running `/solve` or `/hive` session from Telegram by sending CTRL+C to it.

The original PR added a brand-new `/stop` command, but `main` has since gained its own `/stop` command (chat-level pause, #1081). To preserve both behaviours, this PR layers a UUID-aware dispatch in front of the existing `/stop` flow so that:

- `/stop <UUID>` or replying with `/stop` to a message containing a UUID → forwards CTRL+C to the matching isolated session via `$ --stop <uuid>` (link-foundation/start#112).
- Bare `/stop` (optionally with a free-text reason) → keeps its existing chat-pause behaviour from #1081.

This mirrors the UUID-resolution pattern already used by `/log` and `/terminal_watch`.

## Changes

- **`src/isolation-runner.lib.mjs`** — new `stopIsolatedSession(uuid, verbose)` helper wrapping `\$ --stop <uuid>`. Works for any isolation backend (screen, tmux, docker) because `$` knows the backend it launched with. Returns `{ success, output, error }`.
- **`src/telegram-start-stop-command.lib.mjs`**:
  - new `extractStopSessionId(text, repliedTo)` — pure helper that strips the `/stop` (or `/stop@botname`) prefix, then looks for a UUID first in the argument text and second in the replied-to message's `text`/`caption`.
  - `bot.command('stop', …)` now detects UUID modes **before** the forwarded/reply rejection that the chat-pause flow uses, because the reply-of-`/stop` mode is intentionally a reply.
  - Auth model matches `/log`: chat creator in groups; implicit owner in private DMs. Sends an ack message that's then edited with the result.
- **`src/telegram-bot.mjs`** — help text documents the new `/stop <uuid>` mode.
- **`tests/test-issue-524-stop-uuid.mjs`** — 25 assertions covering `extractStopSessionId` (argument vs. reply, prefix stripping, lowercasing, edge cases) and the dispatcher (UUID mode bypasses the reply rejection, bare `/stop` does not call `stopIsolatedSession`, group-creator-only auth, old-message gating, success/error formatting).
- **`.changeset/issue-524-stop-uuid.md`** — patch-level changeset describing the new mode.

## Dependency

Requires the `--stop` flag in the `\$` CLI: link-foundation/start#112. If the binary is missing, the command surfaces a clear error to the user.

## How to reproduce / verify

1. Install `\$` from link-foundation/start (with #112 merged).
2. Start the bot: `npm start` or via the existing scripts.
3. From Telegram, run `/solve <issue url> --isolation screen`. Note the session UUID echoed in the bot's reply.
4. Run `/stop <uuid>` — bot should reply with `⏹️ Asking session …` and then edit to `✅ Stop request sent …`.
5. Alternatively, reply to the original `Executing…` message with `/stop` — same outcome.
6. Run bare `/stop` — chat-pause flow from #1081 still triggers as before.

## Test plan

- [x] `node tests/test-issue-524-stop-uuid.mjs` → 25 passed, 0 failed
- [x] `node tests/test-issue-1686-log-command.mjs` → 45 passed, 0 failed (regression check on the shared UUID extractor)
- [x] `npm run lint` → clean
- [x] `npx prettier --check` on touched files → clean
- [ ] CI green on `issue-524-6aa38eea`

Fixes #524
title: Add /stop <UUID> mode to cancel isolated solve/hive sessions (#524)

body:
## Summary

Closes #1703.

Both the live `displayCostComparison` console output and the `buildCostInfoString` markdown rendered into PR/issue comments collapsed to the short `### 💰 Cost: $X.XXXXXX` form only when the two values matched **exactly** at six decimal places. Real-world calls regularly produce underlying values that differ by ~`1e-7` and round to **adjacent** six-decimal displays (e.g. `$11.219694` vs `$11.219693`), so the rendered `Difference: $-0.000000 (-0.00%)` line carried no information yet still printed three full lines.

The guard now compares the rounded **absolute difference** instead — it fires whenever `|public − anthropic|.toFixed(6) === '0.000000'`. The 1e-6 boundary that the existing slight-difference test exercises is preserved exactly, and the dual-surface (terminal + markdown) invariant introduced by #1557 remains intact.

### Before
```
💰 Cost estimation:
   Public pricing estimate: $11.219694
   Calculated by Anthropic: $11.219693
   Difference:              $-0.000000 (-0.00%)
```

### After
```
💰 Cost: $11.219693
```

## How to reproduce

```js
import Decimal from 'decimal.js-light';

const publicCost = new Decimal('11.21969355');     // → "11.219694" at toFixed(6)
const anthropicCost = new Decimal('11.21969345');  // → "11.219693" at toFixed(6)

publicCost.toFixed(6) === anthropicCost.toFixed(6); // false  ← old guard misses
anthropicCost.minus(publicCost).abs().toFixed(6);   // "0.000000"  ← new guard catches
```

The original triggering log is preserved at `docs/case-studies/issue-1703/raw-data/solution-draft-log-pr-1777377011084.txt`.

## Files changed

- `src/github-cost-info.lib.mjs` — relax the guard to use rounded absolute difference.
- `src/claude.budget-stats.lib.mjs` — same change in the terminal renderer.
- `tests/test-build-cost-info-string.mjs` — three new cases (#1703 main, sub-microcent, $0.000001 boundary).
- `tests/test-display-cost-comparison.mjs` — **new** unit-test file mirroring the buildCostInfoString coverage for the previously-untested terminal renderer.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-1703/` — case study + raw data (gist log, referenced PR comments, issue JSON).
- `.changeset/issue-1703-collapse-cost-section-when-difference-rounds-to-zero.md` — patch-level changeset.

## Test plan

- [x] `node tests/test-build-cost-info-string.mjs` → 55 / 55 PASSED (was 52 / 52)
- [x] `node tests/test-display-cost-comparison.mjs` → 4 / 4 PASSED (new)
- [x] `node tests/test-budget-stats.mjs` → 16 / 16 PASSED (no regression)
- [x] `node tests/test-interactive-mode.mjs` → 111 / 111 PASSED (no regression)
- [x] `npm run lint` → clean
- [x] `npx prettier --check` on all touched files → clean
title: fix(cost): collapse cost section when difference rounds to zero (#1703)

body:
Fixes #1682

## Summary
- Add a guarded `selectLogUploadUrl()` path so Hive Mind refuses to post a log comment when a successful `gh-upload-log` result does not resolve to a usable URL.
- Keep public single-file repository uploads on direct raw URLs, while private repository uploads keep using stable repository/tree URLs instead of short-lived raw links.
- Replace green-check failure-log terminal status with neutral attachment wording so failed runs are not visually marked as successful.
- Add issue #1682 regression coverage and a case-study evidence bundle.

## Reproduction
The captured v1.56.11 run for issue #1680 created PR #1681, failed in Codex, uploaded a 46 MB failure log with `gh-upload-log`, then posted PR comment 4318685883 with `[View complete failure log](null)`. GitHub rendered that relative Markdown target as a broken `.../pull/null` link, and the terminal printed `✅ Failure logs uploaded to Pull Request successfully` immediately before `❌ CODEX execution failed`.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `npm run lint`
- `npm run format:check`
- `GITHUB_BASE_REF=main node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs`
- `node tests/docs-validation.mjs`
- `bash scripts/check-mjs-syntax.sh`
- `bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh`
- `node tests/test-log-upload-output-1682.mjs`

Note: the local environment only has Node v20.20.2 available, while `package.json` declares Node >=24.0.0. The checks above passed under the available local Node.

## Case Study
See `docs/case-studies/issue-1682/README.md` for the timeline, root cause, raw data, compressed full log, and online references.
title: Fix failure log upload links and status

body:
## Summary

Fixes #1712. The `/merge` verbose log read as _"nothing is happening"_ during the legitimate
30-120s gap between GitHub registering a `workflow_run` and publishing its `check_runs`,
and the workflow-run listing showed bare numeric IDs instead of clickable URLs. On
[link-foundation/box#83](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/pull/83) the user watched
the loop for **22 minutes** and Ctrl+C'd because they couldn't verify what `/merge` was
waiting on. The CI ultimately **passed** — the watcher was killed prematurely.

The classification logic was already correct (we sit on top of #1480's grace period); only
the wording and missing URLs were the bug.

### Before

```
[VERBOSE] /merge: PR #83 has no CI checks yet - treating as no_checks
[VERBOSE] /merge:   - Build and Release Docker Image (25097532949): status=pending, conclusion=null
[VERBOSE] /merge: PR #83 has no CI check-runs yet, but 1 workflow run(s) were triggered for SHA dfc4c14 - genuine race condition (waiting for check-runs to appear)
  ⏳ Waiting for CI:         Build and Release Docker Image
```

### After

```
[VERBOSE] /merge: PR #83 commit dfc4c14 has no check-runs or commit statuses registered yet (status=no_checks; race vs. no-CI distinction is decided downstream)
[VERBOSE] /merge: Found 1 workflow run(s) for SHA dfc4c14
[VERBOSE] /merge:   - Build and Release Docker Image (run #25097532949): status=pending, conclusion=null — https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25097532949
[VERBOSE] /merge: PR #83 commit dfc4c14 has 1 workflow run(s) registered, but check-runs have not been published yet — waiting for the runs to publish check-runs:
[VERBOSE] /merge:   - Build and Release Docker Image (run #25097532949): status=pending, conclusion=null — https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25097532949
  ⏳ Waiting for CI:         Build and Release Docker Image [pending] — https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25097532949
```

The user-facing `⏳ Waiting for CI:` line now contains a URL the user can paste into the
browser to verify the run state in <5 seconds.

## Changes

### `src/github-merge.lib.mjs`
- `getDetailedCIStatus` and `checkPRCIStatus` reword the `no_checks` verbose log to
  *"has no check-runs or commit statuses registered yet"* including the short SHA.
- `getWorkflowRunsForSha` verbose listing now appends `run.html_url` to every entry.
- Normalized check-run / commit-status entries carry an `html_url` field (falling back
  to `details_url` / `target_url`).

### `src/solve.auto-merge-helpers.lib.mjs::getMergeBlockers`
- **`no_checks` branch**: rewords the verbose message; per-run `[status/conclusion] —
  url` is logged for each workflow run; blocker `details` strings are
  `"<name> [<status>] — <url>"`. The user-facing `⏳ Waiting for CI: …` line in
  `solve.auto-merge.lib.mjs` (which joins `details` with commas) automatically picks
  up the URL.
- **`pending` branch**: same enrichment for check-runs that exist but are still
  running/queued.
- **`cancelled` branch**: details now include conclusion + URL.

### Tests
`tests/test-misleading-merge-logs-1712.mjs` — 13 unit tests, all passing:
- Wording sanity (the literal phrase _"has no CI checks yet"_ is gone)
- Blocker enrichment for `no_checks` / `pending` / `cancelled` paths includes URL + status
- Regression guard for #1466 (action_required-only runs still go to `noCiTriggered`)
- Joined user-facing line format

### Case study
`docs/case-studies/issue-1712/README.md` — full case study with raw API snapshots
(PR, workflow runs, check-runs), reconstructed timeline, root cause, fix description,
and verification on the original case (CI ultimately passed —
`check-runs-dfc4c14.json` shows `total_count: 22`).

## Test plan

- [x] `node tests/test-misleading-merge-logs-1712.mjs` — 13 / 13 pass
- [x] `node tests/test-false-positive-workflow-run-race-1480.mjs` — 31 / 31 pass (no regression)
- [x] `node tests/test-action-required-ci-stuck-1466.mjs` — 14 / 14 pass (no regression)
- [x] `npm run lint` clean
- [x] Manual verification with the recorded data: blocker `details[0]` becomes
  `"Build and Release Docker Image [in_progress] — https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25097532949"`

## Upstream issues

None — the bug was entirely in `link-assistant/hive-mind`. The external workflow on
`link-foundation/box#83` finished successfully after the watcher was killed.
title: fix(merge): list CI across all PR commits, dedupe logs, commit URLs + status hints (#1712)

body:
## 🎯 Summary

This PR fixes pricing calculation failures during Pull Request comment generation in the solve command, as reported in issue #667.

## 🐛 Problem

During auto-restart scenarios, the pricing information displayed in PR comments showed "unknown" for one or both pricing sources, even though the actual data was calculated correctly and appeared in logs.

### Two Distinct Failure Modes

1. **Missing Anthropic Cost Data** (First run)
   - Public pricing estimate: ✅ Works
   - Calculated by Anthropic: ❌ Shows "unknown"

2. **Missing Public Pricing Data** (Auto-restart runs)
   - Public pricing estimate: ❌ Shows "unknown"
   - Calculated by Anthropic: ✅ Works

### Evidence

Referenced [PR #49](https://github.com/konard/hh-job-application-automation/pull/49) which exhibited both failure modes.

## 🔍 Root Cause

**Session variable scope mismatch** between main execution and auto-restart iterations:

1. Main `solve.mjs` runs first Claude session → captures `sessionId` and `anthropicTotalCostUSD`
2. Auto-restart triggers → `solve.watch.lib.mjs` runs NEW Claude session → creates new session data
3. Watch mode completes → **NEW session data NOT returned** to main flow
4. Log upload uses **OLD session variables** → reads wrong file or missing data → shows "unknown"

## ✅ Solution Implemented

Track and return the latest session data from watch mode iterations to ensure accurate pricing display.

### Changes Made

1. **`src/solve.watch.lib.mjs`**
   - Added tracking of `latestSessionId` and `latestAnthropicCost` across all watch iterations
   - Return these values from `watchForFeedback()` function
   - Modified `startWatchMode()` to return the session data

2. **`src/solve.mjs`**
   - Changed `sessionId` and `anthropicTotalCostUSD` from const to let declarations
   - Capture return value from `startWatchMode()`
   - Update session variables with latest data before log upload
   - Added verbose logging to track session data updates

3. **Testing**
   - Added `experiments/test-session-tracking.mjs` to verify tracking logic
   - All ESLint checks pass (0 errors, 11 pre-existing warnings)

## 📊 Results

### Before
```
💰 **Cost estimation:**
- Public pricing estimate: unknown
- Calculated by Anthropic: $0.964416 USD
- Difference: unknown
```

### After
```
💰 **Cost estimation:**
- Public pricing estimate: $1.615948 USD
- Calculated by Anthropic: $0.964416 USD
- Difference: $-0.651532 (-40.32%)
```

## 📁 Documentation

Complete case study available in: `docs/case-studies/issue-667-pricing-calculation-failures/`

- **README.md** - Comprehensive analysis with evidence and solutions
- **TECHNICAL_SUMMARY.md** - Quick reference for developers
- **pr49-run1-log.txt** - Evidence from first run
- **pr49-run2-log.txt** - Evidence from auto-restart

## 🧪 Testing

- ✅ Session tracking logic verified with experiment script
- ✅ ESLint validation passed
- ✅ Both pricing sources now use correct session data
- ✅ Works for single runs and auto-restart scenarios
- ✅ Backward compatible with existing functionality

## 🎓 Why This Matters

Both pricing values are important:
1. **Public Pricing** (models.dev): External validation, transparency
2. **Anthropic Official**: Actual billing amount, authoritative

Showing "unknown" reduces user trust and makes cost tracking impossible.

## 🔗 Related

- Fixes #667
- Referenced PR: [konard/hh-job-application-automation#49](https://github.com/konard/hh-job-application-automation/pull/49)

---

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
title: Fix pricing calculation failures at PR generation (#667)

body:
## Problem

When Claude Code's `<persisted-output>` feature truncates a large tool output, the cut point can fall between the two UTF-16 code units of an emoji surrogate pair (e.g. 🤖 = `\uD83E\uDD16`). The orphaned high surrogate (`\uD83E`) is then passed to `JSON.stringify()`, which emits it as-is. The Anthropic API (correctly, per RFC 8259 §7) rejects the payload with:

```
API Error: 400 {"type":"error","error":{"type":"invalid_request_error","message":"The request body is not valid JSON: no low surrogate in string: line 1 column 28642 (char 28641)"}}
```

This makes the session permanently broken — every subsequent turn re-sends the same invalid JSON.

## Root Cause

**The root cause is inside the Claude Code CLI binary** (its `<persisted-output>` truncation logic), which is outside hive-mind's control. The bug has been reported upstream: [anthropics/claude-code#16294](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/16294).

## Important context: the original issue did NOT use `--interactive-mode`

From the original issue log:
```
node /home/hive/.bun/bin/solve https://github.com/MILANA808/Milana-backend/issues/23 --model opus --attach-logs --verbose --no-tool-check --auto-resume-on-limit-reset --auto-restart-until-mergeable --tokens-budget-stats
```

The command was run **without** `--interactive-mode`. The previous fix only covered `interactive-mode.lib.mjs`, but the orphaned surrogates affect **all** CLI output parsing paths. This update extends the sanitization to every path where hive-mind parses NDJSON output from CLI tools.

## Solution

### Shared sanitization module: `unicode-sanitization.lib.mjs`

Extracted `sanitizeUnicode(text)` and `sanitizeObjectStrings(value)` into a dedicated shared module:

- **`sanitizeUnicode(text)`** — replaces every orphaned surrogate (high not followed by low, or low not preceded by high) with U+FFFD
- **`sanitizeObjectStrings(value)`** — recursively sanitizes all string values in an object/array, preserving non-string values

### Applied in all CLI output parsing paths

Every `JSON.parse(line)` call that processes NDJSON output from CLI tools now passes through `sanitizeObjectStrings()`:

| File | What it parses | Lines sanitized |
|------|---------------|----------------|
| `src/claude.lib.mjs` | Claude CLI NDJSON stdout (main + buffer remainder) | 2 call sites |
| `src/agent.lib.mjs` | Agent SDK NDJSON stdout + stderr | 4 call sites |
| `src/codex.lib.mjs` | Codex CLI NDJSON stdout | 1 call site |
| `src/opencode.lib.mjs` | OpenCode CLI NDJSON stdout + stderr | 2 call sites |
| `src/interactive-mode.lib.mjs` | Imports from shared module (was previously inline) | `truncateMiddle()`, `safeJsonStringify()` |

### What this protects

- **Log serialization** (`JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)`) — no invalid JSON in logs
- **Result summary capture** (`data.result` → posted as PR comment) — no orphaned surrogates in GitHub API calls
- **Error detection patterns** (`.includes('Session limit reached')`, etc.) — still work after sanitization (verified by tests)
- **Cost/token data** — numeric fields preserved unchanged through sanitization

## Unit Tests

### New: `tests/test-unicode-sanitization.mjs` (62 tests)

| Test Category | Count | What it verifies |
|--------------|-------|-----------------|
| Basic sanitizeUnicode | 8 | Null/undefined/empty, ASCII, BMP Unicode, valid emoji |
| Orphaned surrogate handling | 11 | High/low orphans at start/end/middle, reversed pairs, range boundaries |
| Real-world Claude output patterns | 10 | Issue #1324 error, emoji-rich content, Cyrillic/CJK, persisted-output truncation, idempotency |
| sanitizeObjectStrings basics | 9 | Primitives, flat/nested objects, arrays, mixed types |
| Claude NDJSON event simulation | 7 | assistant, result, tool_result, tool_use, system.init, deeply nested, multi-content |
| JSON round-trip safety | 5 | String/object/complex round-trips, pathological surrogates |
| Simulated regular Claude parsing | 8 | NDJSON line parsing, cost preservation, message counting, error detection (session limit, overload, 503, timeout, result summary) |
| Performance/stress | 2 | 100KB text, 50-level nesting |

### Existing: `tests/test-interactive-mode.mjs` (80 tests) — all passing

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/unicode-sanitization.lib.mjs` | **New** — shared `sanitizeUnicode()` + `sanitizeObjectStrings()` |
| `src/claude.lib.mjs` | Import + apply `sanitizeObjectStrings` at both `JSON.parse` sites |
| `src/agent.lib.mjs` | Import + apply `sanitizeObjectStrings` at 4 `JSON.parse` sites |
| `src/codex.lib.mjs` | Import + apply `sanitizeObjectStrings` at `JSON.parse` site |
| `src/opencode.lib.mjs` | Import + apply `sanitizeObjectStrings` at 2 `JSON.parse` sites |
| `src/interactive-mode.lib.mjs` | Replace inline `sanitizeUnicode` with import from shared module |
| `tests/test-unicode-sanitization.mjs` | **New** — 62 unit tests |
| `.github/workflows/release.yml` | Add CI step for Unicode sanitization tests |
| `.changeset/fix-unicode-surrogate-sanitization.md` | Updated changeset |

Full root cause analysis, timeline, and reproducible example documented in:
- `docs/case-studies/issue-1324/CASE-STUDY.md`
- `docs/case-studies/issue-1324/REPRODUCIBLE-EXAMPLE.md`

title: fix: sanitize orphaned UTF-16 surrogates to prevent Anthropic API 400 errors (Issue #1324)
